fundamentals.name: AppLovin Corporation
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: AppLovin Corporation provides end-to-end artificial intelligence-powered advertising solutions for businesses in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Advertising and Apps. The company offers Axon Ads Manager, a suite of marketing solutions that enables developers to automate, optimize, and manage marketing efforts; MAX, an in-app bidding technology that optimizes the value of a publisher's advertising inventory by running a real-time competitive auction; Adjust, a measurement and analytics marketing platform; and Wurl, a connected TV platform, which distributes streaming video for content companies, provides advertising and publishing solutions. It serves individuals, small and independent businesses, enterprises, advertisers and advertising networks, mobile app publishers, and indie studio developers. The company was incorporated in 2011 and is headquartered in Palo Alto, California.

judgment.summary: High-growth, highly profitable company with strong analyst conviction and a premium valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 3.4% (3.56 vs 3.44); next earnings date is 2026-08-05.
positives: Revenue growth 59.0% YoY and earnings growth 113.1% YoY indicate strong top and bottom-line expansion.
Profit margin 64.3% and operating margin 78.2% are exceptionally high, demonstrating operational efficiency.
Return on equity at 266.4% is outstanding, showing effective use of shareholder capital.
Free cash flow of $3.18B TTM supports operations and potential shareholder returns.
Analyst consensus is 'strong buy' with a mean target of $648.10, suggesting significant upside potential.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 52.07 and Price/Sales of 32.69 indicate a very high valuation.
PEG ratio of 1.56 suggests growth may not fully justify the current P/E multiple.
Debt/Equity ratio of 162.89 is high, though offset by strong cash flow and current ratios.
Beta of 2.37 indicates higher volatility compared to the broader market.

news.paragraph: AppLovin (APP) saw significant positive attention this week, with multiple outlets reporting on a bullish call and price target hike that propelled the stock up over 10%. Analysts are resetting forecasts, with one noting a 'hidden metric' while others maintain a 'strong buy' consensus and see further upside. This follows the launch of the Trump Accounts app, which has also driven gains for Robinhood (HOOD), though APP's news is primarily focused on its core business and analyst sentiment.

headlines.title: HOOD Stock Jumps To Best Day This Month On Trump Accounts App Launch
